SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.util.heattransfer

Class BoilOffCalculator

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.util.heattransfer.BoilOffCalculator
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class BoilOffCalculator extends Object implements Serializable
Steady-state boil-off (heat-ingress) calculator for insulated cryogenic or refrigerated vessels.

Heat leaking through the insulation evaporates the stored liquid and sets the minimum relief (boil-off) rate that the vent or pressure-relief system must accommodate. The series thermal resistance of the outer film and the insulation layer gives an effective heat-transfer coefficient

$$ h_{eff} = \left( \frac{1}{h_o} + \frac{t_{ins}}{k_{ins}} \right)^{-1} $$

from which the heat ingress Q = h_eff * A * (T_amb - T_fluid) and the latent-heat boil-off rate m_dot = Q / L follow. Sweeping the insulation thickness reproduces the familiar BoilFAST-style trade-off between insulation thickness and relief load.

References: ISO 21013; API 521 §5.15 (heat leak); BoilFAST methodology.

Version:
1.0
Author:
ESOL
See Also:

    BoilOffCalculator()

  • Constructor Details

    • BoilOffCalculator

      public BoilOffCalculator()
      Creates a boil-off calculator with default values representative of an LNG storage application.

  • Method Details

    • setOuterFilmCoefficient

      public BoilOffCalculator setOuterFilmCoefficient(double outerFilmCoefficient)
      Sets the outer-surface film heat-transfer coefficient.
      Parameters:
      outerFilmCoefficient - outer film coefficient in W/(m²·K); must be positive
      Returns:
      this calculator for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if outerFilmCoefficient is not positive

    • setInsulationConductivity

      public BoilOffCalculator setInsulationConductivity(double insulationConductivity)
      Sets the insulation thermal conductivity.
      Parameters:
      insulationConductivity - insulation conductivity in W/(m·K); must be positive
      Returns:
      this calculator for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if insulationConductivity is not positive

    • setSurfaceArea

      public BoilOffCalculator setSurfaceArea(double surfaceAreaM2)
      Sets the heat-transfer surface area.
      Parameters:
      surfaceAreaM2 - surface area in m²; must be positive
      Returns:
      this calculator for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if surfaceAreaM2 is not positive

    • setAmbientTemperatureK

      public BoilOffCalculator setAmbientTemperatureK(double ambientTemperatureK)
      Sets the ambient temperature.
      Parameters:
      ambientTemperatureK - ambient temperature in K; must be positive
      Returns:
      this calculator for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if ambientTemperatureK is not positive

    • setFluidTemperatureK

      public BoilOffCalculator setFluidTemperatureK(double fluidTemperatureK)
      Sets the stored-fluid (boiling) temperature.
      Parameters:
      fluidTemperatureK - fluid temperature in K; must be positive
      Returns:
      this calculator for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if fluidTemperatureK is not positive

    • setLatentHeat

      public BoilOffCalculator setLatentHeat(double latentHeatJPerKg)
      Sets the latent heat of vaporization of the stored fluid.
      Parameters:
      latentHeatJPerKg - latent heat in J/kg; must be positive
      Returns:
      this calculator for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if latentHeatJPerKg is not positive

    • effectiveHeatTransferCoefficient

      public double effectiveHeatTransferCoefficient(double insulationThicknessM)
      Computes the effective heat-transfer coefficient for a given insulation thickness.
      Parameters:
      insulationThicknessM - insulation thickness in m; must be non-negative
      Returns:
      effective heat-transfer coefficient in W/(m²·K)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if insulationThicknessM is negative

    • heatIngressW

      public double heatIngressW(double insulationThicknessM)
      Computes the steady heat ingress through the insulation.
      Parameters:
      insulationThicknessM - insulation thickness in m; must be non-negative
      Returns:
      heat ingress in W (positive into the cold fluid)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if insulationThicknessM is negative

    • boilOffRateKgPerS

      public double boilOffRateKgPerS(double insulationThicknessM)
      Computes the latent-heat boil-off (relief) rate.
      Parameters:
      insulationThicknessM - insulation thickness in m; must be non-negative
      Returns:
      boil-off rate in kg/s
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if insulationThicknessM is negative

    • boilOffRateKgPerH

      public double boilOffRateKgPerH(double insulationThicknessM)
      Computes the latent-heat boil-off (relief) rate per hour.
      Parameters:
      insulationThicknessM - insulation thickness in m; must be non-negative
      Returns:
      boil-off rate in kg/h
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if insulationThicknessM is negative

    • sweepInsulationThickness

      public double[][] sweepInsulationThickness(double minThicknessM, double maxThicknessM, int points)
      Sweeps a range of insulation thicknesses and returns the corresponding boil-off rates.
      Parameters:
      minThicknessM - minimum insulation thickness in m; must be non-negative
      maxThicknessM - maximum insulation thickness in m; must be greater than minThicknessM
      points - number of evenly spaced points; must be at least two
      Returns:
      a two-row array: [0] thicknesses in m, [1] boil-off rates in kg/h
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the range or point count is invalid
